stem: Which composable enforces that only one item can be selected at a time?
feedback: createSingle extends createSelection to cap selection at a single item — that's the enforcement. createSelection allows multiple; createGroup adds batch/tri-state; createModel is the value store beneath them.

stem: Which composable is the multi-select foundation that the others extend?
feedback: createSelection is the multi-select base. createSingle, createGroup, and createNested all build on it; createModel is the lower-level value store beneath it.

stem: You need select-all with an indeterminate (tri-state) parent checkbox. Which composable?
feedback: createGroup extends createSelection with batch operations and tri-state support — exactly the select-all/indeterminate pattern.

stem: Which composable manages hierarchical, parent/child (tree) selection?
feedback: createNested extends createGroup for tree structures — treeviews, nested menus, and cascading parent/child selection.

stem: Which of these are built on the selection system? (Select all that apply)
feedback: createSingle, createGroup, and createNested all extend the selection stack. The rest are unrelated — useBreakpoints is a responsive plugin, createRegistry/createContext are foundation primitives, createPagination/createFilter are data composables.

stem: Which composable is the id-based value store that sits beneath the selection composables?
feedback: createModel is the value store — it holds the id-keyed values that createSelection and its descendants layer selection semantics on top of.

stem: You're building a wizard with next/prev/first/last step navigation. Which composable?
feedback: createStep extends createSingle with first/last/next/prev/step methods — the natural fit for wizards and carousels.

stem: Which createSelection option prevents the user from deselecting the last selected item?
feedback: mandatory enforces that at least one item stays selected — deselecting the last one is blocked. multiple toggles multi-select; disabled turns the whole selection off.

stem: Which createSelection option turns it into a multi-select?
feedback: Pass multiple: true to allow more than one item selected at once. Without it, createSelection still permits multiple — createSingle is what caps it to one.

stem: createSingle is built by extending which composable?
feedback: createSingle extends createSelection, narrowing it to a single-item selection. createStep in turn extends createSingle.

stem: createNested adds tree structure by extending which composable?
feedback: createNested extends createGroup, inheriting its batch and tri-state operations before layering on parent/child hierarchy.

stem: Which foundational composable manages the collection of items (tickets) that selection builds on?
feedback: createRegistry is the item/ticket collection primitive. Selection composables register their items as tickets in a registry, then track which ids are selected.
